how_computed: Percentage of gold (sprint x field) cells where the agent's emitted dashboard field equals the deterministic gold field. Numeric fields (defect_density, rolling_avg_3_sprint, deviation_pct) match within 0.005; integer counts and alert_flag match exactly; sprint_name and trend match as exact strings. A field the agent omitted counts as a mismatch. Denominator = sprints x 10 fields.
rationale: Every agent receives the same six sprint fixtures (Jira bug list + git numstat + three preceding densities) and must compute the exact published dashboard record. Unlike the contract-testing tasks, here the agent does the arithmetic, so the metric measures which framework most reliably produces the correct defect-density report: counting P1-P4 by exact priority, excluding *test.go/*test.py/*.spec.ts before summing changed lines, dividing for density, rolling-averaging, computing the deviation, applying the strictly-greater-than-20% alert rule, and formatting the signed trend. The fixtures deliberately span an all-clear sprint, a density-spike alert, a P1-only escalation, a combined alert+P1, an improving negative-trend sprint, and an exactly-20%% boundary (alert false) with heavy test-file exclusion, so a framework that fumbles any rule loses cells.
pass_threshold: accuracy = 100% (agent reproduces every gold field on every sprint)
fail_condition: accuracy < 100% (any miscount, mis-filtered line, rounding/sign error, boundary mistake, or omitted field)
